metadata.description: Transporter configuration including physics, task areas, and start positions

fields.model: Transporter model type ('2D' or '3D') - determines which physics section to use
fields.start_station: Station number where transporter begins operation

physics_2D.description: Physics parameters for 2D transporters (X and Z axes only)
physics_2D.x_acceleration_time_s: Time in seconds to reach maximum speed in X direction
physics_2D.x_deceleration_time_s: Time in seconds to decelerate from maximum speed to stop in X direction
physics_2D.x_max_speed_mm_s: Maximum X-axis travel speed in millimeters per second
physics_2D.z_slow_distance_dry_mm: Initial slow distance for dry stations in mm
physics_2D.z_slow_distance_wet_mm: Initial slow distance for wet stations in mm
physics_2D.z_slow_end_distance_mm: Final slow approach/departure distance in mm
physics_2D.z_slow_speed_mm_s: Slow vertical movement speed in mm/s
physics_2D.z_fast_speed_mm_s: Fast vertical movement speed in mm/s
physics_2D.drip_tray_delay_s: Time to open/close drip tray in seconds
physics_2D.avoid_distance_mm: Minimum collision avoidance distance in mm

physics_3D.description: Physics parameters for 3D transporters (X, Y, and Z axes)
physics_3D.x_acceleration_time_s: Time in seconds to reach maximum speed in X direction
physics_3D.x_deceleration_time_s: Time in seconds to decelerate from maximum speed to stop in X direction
physics_3D.x_max_speed_mm_s: Maximum X-axis travel speed in millimeters per second
physics_3D.y_acceleration_time_s: Time in seconds to reach maximum speed in Y direction
physics_3D.y_deceleration_time_s: Time in seconds to decelerate from maximum speed to stop in Y direction
physics_3D.y_max_speed_mm_s: Maximum Y-axis travel speed in millimeters per second
physics_3D.z_total_distance_mm: Maximum Z-axis height (physical limit) in millimeters
physics_3D.z_idle_height_mm: Height for empty transporter movement/transfer in millimeters
physics_3D.z_speed_change_limit_dry_mm: Height where dry stations switch from slow to fast speed in mm
physics_3D.z_speed_change_limit_wet_mm: Height where wet stations switch from slow to fast speed in mm
physics_3D.z_slow_end_distance_mm: Final slow approach/departure distance in mm
physics_3D.z_slow_speed_mm_s: Slow vertical movement speed in mm/s
physics_3D.z_fast_speed_mm_s: Fast vertical movement speed in mm/s
physics_3D.drip_tray_delay_s: Time to open/close drip tray in seconds
physics_3D.catcher_delay_s: Time to engage/release catchers in seconds
physics_3D.avoid_distance_mm: Minimum collision avoidance distance in mm

task_areas.description: Defines operational boundaries for each production line

line_xxx.min_lift_station: Lowest station number where transporter can lift loads
line_xxx.max_lift_station: Highest station number where transporter can lift loads
line_xxx.min_sink_station: Lowest station number where transporter can lower loads
line_xxx.max_sink_station: Highest station number where transporter can lower loads

notes: 2D transporters typically serve only one line (one line_xxx with non-zero values)
3D transporters can serve multiple lines (multiple line_xxx with non-zero values)
Station numbers with value 0 indicate the line is not operational or not assigned to this transporter
Station numbers with value 0 indicate the line is not operational or not assigned to this transporter
Task areas define operational zones - transporters cannot operate outside their assigned min/max ranges
Physics parameters are critical for accurate movement time calculations in simulation
2D transporters serve a single line, 3D transporters can serve multiple lines
